summary: We recommend seeding D2 with 20 hand-crafted pairs each targeting one specific failure mode of the HeuristicVerifier so D3 can attribute confusion matrix entries to a named rubric axis. The tradeoff against using real-world low-quality PRs is that targeted fixtures cannot capture the long tail of stylistic badness; the goal of D3 is to validate that each rubric dimension fires in isolation which targeted fixtures achieve. All 20 pairs verified strictly below the canonical floor with a score range of 1.4 to 3.4 and a MANIFEST md records each pair score and target dimension.

claims.statement: outcomes-mcp pull 14 adds 40 files to evals pairs failing representing 20 outcome and report pairs because the PR diff lists exactly 40 new pair files plus the MANIFEST md.

citations.title: evals pairs failing after the seed

claims.caveats: Pairs scoring near 3.4 are intentionally close to the floor; if D5 ratchets the floor up to 4.0 these pairs remain failing.

claims.statement: all 20 pairs verified to score strictly below 3.5 because the MANIFEST md verification log records 20 of 20 scoring below the floor with a range of 1.4 to 3.4 on 2026-05-22.
claims.caveats: Six initial drafts scored at or above 3.5 and were rewritten until they fired below the floor.

open_questions: Should D2 also add a separate parse-failure bucket for pairs that fail at schema validation before scoring?
methodology_notes: Inspected sdk-python verifier thresholds then authored 20 pairs each targeting one threshold; 6 pairs that initially scored above 3.5 were rewritten until all 20 fired below the floor.
